examples: It must be Piney that sent us this present.
Dioryctria rubella Hampson is an important insect pest of the piney shoot, bud, cone and trunk.
And beyond them to a sweep of fields and flat piney woods that stretched to the western horizon.
When rubbed between the fingers it gave off a warm, strong, piney smell, a bit like floor polish.
We fall asleep to the same haunting calls of the loons and the same piney smell of the New Hampshire woods.
